2025-04-04.thesis: China imposes export controls on medium and heavy rare earth materials
2025-04-04.exit_case: China's export controls officially announced April 4; catalyst event already occurred.
2025-04-04.assessment: China officially imposed export controls on medium and heavy rare earths on April 4, triggering immediate market reaction with U.S. rare earth stocks like MP Materials gaining attention as domestic alternatives. Coverage remains focused on the tariff and supply chain impacts, but the catalyst event is now public and appears digested; no concrete follow-on catalyst is identified yet.
2025-04-04.exit_advice: Position should be closed as the specific catalyst (export controls imposition) has occurred and been publicly announced, eliminating the early edge. The event is resolved and likely priced in given broad media coverage and market reaction.

Morgan Overweight call from prior week still anchors the positive setup. Silence decay applies: one week with no fresh LLY coverage steps conviction down modestly.
2025-08-01.exit_advice: Hold through earnings next week. The catalyst is days away — exiting now forfeits the binary event entirely. If the report disappoints or is already priced in post-report on Aug 7, reassess immediately.

2025-08-01.exit_case: The US-EU trade deal has been officially struck and announced, resolving the catalyst.

2025-08-08.thesis: LLY Q2 earnings report scheduled August 7, 2025
2025-08-08.exit_case: LLY Q2 earnings reported August 7 — the acute catalyst has occurred and passed.
2025-08-08.assessment: LLY Q2 earnings were scheduled for August 7 and that date has now passed (week ending August 8). The news flow this week contains zero LLY-specific earnings coverage — no results, no beat/miss analysis, no guidance headlines — which is unusual but irrelevant to the exit decision. The binary fact is simple: the catalyst I entered on was the earnings report itself, and August 7 has come and gone. This was always an acute, single-date catalyst, not a structural driver. Once the report is filed, the edge (pre-positioning ahead of a known catalyst) is fully spent. The J.P. Morgan Overweight setup that anchored conviction was a pre-earnings positioning thesis; it cannot survive the actual report. There is no scheduled follow-on event within this catalyst's scope. Mark resolved, close, and move on.
2025-08-08.exit_advice: The earnings date is behind us. This was a single datable event that has now occurred. There is no concrete next catalyst in this event's scope. Close the position and free capital for live pending catalysts.

2025-09-12.thesis: California considering payment to prevent Valero Benicia refinery shutdown
2025-09-12.exit_case: none — discussions just surfaced; no payment made, no bill filed, no deal signed.
2025-09-12.assessment: Week 1 of the California-Benicia catalyst. Legislators are in early-stage discussions to pay Valero hundreds of millions to keep its Benicia refinery operational. This is sourced via Bloomberg/local outlet, still pre-legislation/pre-deal — very early, under-the-radar. VLO is the direct, clean pure-play. Multiple concrete milestones lie ahead: formal legislative proposal, potential vote, deal terms, environmental/political opposition hearings. Benicia refinery matters for CA fuel supply/rack prices, and a state cash injection would be a direct margin tailwind for VLO. Early but uncertain — political outcome not guaranteed.
2025-09-12.exit_advice: HOLD — catalyst is live and pre-resolution. Watch for: (1) formal bill introduction, (2) legislative committee vote, (3) any opposition from environmental groups or competing refiners, (4) actual dollar figures or terms. If discussions collapse or the legislature signals no appetite, exit. If a bill advances, conviction climbs.

2025-09-19.exit_advice: Hold — catalyst is still live with upcoming legislative and deal milestones; silence does not imply resolution.

2025-10-10.thesis: California considering payment to prevent Valero Benicia refinery shutdown
2025-10-10.exit_case: Five weeks of zero legislative activity on an acute initiative suggests the payment idea quietly died in early discussions, though unconfirmed.
2025-10-10.assessment: Week 5 — fifth consecutive week with no fresh coverage. The acute legislative initiative to pay Valero to keep Benicia open has shown zero formal progress: no proposal introduced, no hearing scheduled, no party publicly championing it. Silence decay compounds to conviction 3, now at cull-threshold. The absence of any follow-through after the initial Bloomberg report increasingly suggests the idea stalled in early discussions. However, neither formal rejection nor the refinery announcing plans to stay has occurred — the driving condition has not publicly resolved or reversed.
2025-10-10.exit_advice: This acute political initiative has generated five weeks of zero progress — it is functionally stalled. Silence decay pushes conviction to 2 next week if no fresh coverage arrives, which is definite cull territory. No concrete milestone is scheduled or announced (speculative 'maybe the legislature returns to it' doesn't count). The position should be culled now or at next rebalance unless a fresh legislative activity report surfaces. Replace with a higher-conviction event.

2025-10-10.thesis: FTSE Russell secondary-emerging-market upgrade, effective Sep 21 2026 pending Mar interim review
2025-10-10.exit_case: Announcement already caused record-high spike; actual inclusion and passive flows still 11+ months away with interim review execution risk.
2025-10-10.assessment: FTSE Russell officially announced Vietnam's upgrade to secondary emerging market status on Oct 8, effective Sep 21 2026 pending a March 2026 interim review confirming market-access improvements. Vietnam's benchmark index hit a record high on the news, and the government publicly welcomed it. The announcement pop is done, but the real passive-flow catalysts are still ahead — the March 2026 interim review is a concrete checkpoint that could confirm or stall the upgrade, and the actual inclusion date is ~11 months out. The playbook from prior FTSE upgrades (e.g., Pakistan, Romania) shows the biggest inflows cluster around the effective date, not the announcement. VNM remains the cleanest vehicle for Vietnam equity exposure through this event. The long runway means the trade needs patience, but the concrete milestones ahead keep it live.
2025-10-10.exit_advice: Hold through the announcement pop. The announcement was the trigger but NOT the resolution — actual index inclusion (Sep 2026) and the March interim review are concrete milestones still ahead with real flow implications. Re-anchor to the March 2026 review as the next checkpoint. Trim only if Vietnam fails the interim review or broader EM exodus overwhelms.

2025-10-17.assessment: The FTSE Russell secondary-emerging-market upgrade for Vietnam is still scheduled, with no news this week. The catalyst is live, and the long runway to the effective date means passive inflows are still ahead, though silence suggests fading attention.
2025-10-17.exit_advice: Hold position as the FTSE Russell upgrade for Vietnam remains pending with concrete milestones ahead: the March 2026 interim review and the September 2026 effective date.

2025-10-17.thesis: China rare-earth export curbs + Nov 1 tariff threat drives MP Materials higher
2025-10-17.exit_case: none — curbs active, Nov 1 tariff threat still ahead; no reversal or resolution yet.
2025-10-17.assessment: This is the first week of a fresh, specific catalyst. On Oct 13, Beijing unveiled export curbs on rare earths and Trump threatened an additional 100% tariff on China effective Nov 1 (Japan Times, multiple outlets confirmed). The export curbs are now IN EFFECT and the tariff deadline is a concrete, date-certain catalyst still two weeks away. MP Materials is the cleanest US rare-earth pure play; REMX provides broad basket exposure; UUUU has rare-earth processing upside alongside its uranium business; ALB is the weakest link (primarily lithium) but still rides the critical-minerals supply-chain narrative. Multiple Oct 13 articles confirm acute US-China trade escalation with rare earths as the focal point. This is early, specific, under-the-radar relative to the magnitude, and has a clear pending catalyst (Nov 1). Structurally, China's rare-earth curbs could be open-ended — even after Nov 1 tariff resolution (or escalation), the supply constraint persists. Hold through to Nov 1 and beyond as long as curbs remain in force.
2025-10-17.exit_advice: China rare-earth export curbs are in effect and NOT reversed; Nov 1 tariff deadline is concrete and still ~2 weeks away. No exit case exists yet. Watch for: (1) Nov 1 arrival and actual tariff imposition/escalation/share, (2) any negotiation that lifts the rare-earth export curbs as the true reversal signal. Silence between now and Nov 1 is normal — do NOT fade on quiet days; the deadline itself is the catalyst event.

2025-11-21.thesis: IAEA resolution against Iran escalates uranium supply concerns
2025-11-21.exit_case: Resolution has not yet been voted — it remains Western draft-stage; Iran has only issued a warning so far.
2025-11-21.assessment: First week. The IAEA Board of Governors is convening amid a Western push for a resolution censuring Iran, but the vote has NOT yet occurred — the catalyst is still pending. Iran has publicly warned the US and E3 against the draft (Nov 16). Nuclear stocks are appearing on generic screener watchlists (Nov 12-19), but coverage is broad 'stocks to watch' fluff, not deep analysis linking the specific IAEA vote to uranium supply disruption. The edge is the under-appreciated supply-chain second-order: a censure vote could trigger Iranian retaliation (restricting IAEA inspectors further, accelerating enrichment), tightening already-structurally-short uranium markets. LEU (Centrus, enriched uranium pure-play) and CCJ (Cameco, largest Western uranium miner) are the cleanest expressions. BWXT, OKLO, and SMR are broader nuclear-exposure beneficiaries. Waiting for the actual vote to catalyze.
2025-11-21.exit_advice: HOLD. IAEA resolution vote is imminent but has not occurred. If passed, expect uranium supply repricing and further Iranian escalation (inspector access cuts, enrichment acceleration). Key watch: vote outcome, Iran's immediate response, any sanctions follow-through.

2025-11-28.thesis: California considering payment to prevent Valero Benicia refinery shutdown
2025-11-28.exit_case: Acute legislative initiative never formalized — 13 weeks, zero proposals, hearings, or champions; early discussions lapsed into silence.
2025-11-28.assessment: Week 13 — thirteen consecutive weeks of total silence on the California-Benicia Valero payment initiative. The initial Bloomberg report described 'early-stage discussions' that have produced zero follow-through: no legislative proposal, no hearing scheduled, no public champion, no deal terms, no political momentum whatsoever. This was an acute legislative initiative, not a structural driver — and an acute catalyst that was never even formally introduced and has shown no pulse for three months is functionally dead. No fresh news this week relates to the Benicia initiative (Oklo articles are entirely unrelated). The position has been at floor conviction since Week 5 and should be formally closed.
2025-11-28.exit_advice: Close position. The acute legislative initiative to pay Valero to keep Benicia open has produced zero follow-through in 13 weeks — no proposal, no hearing, no champion. The 'early-stage discussions' reported in September ended without reaching formal stage. This is not a structural driver that can re-energize; it was a one-shot initiative that never launched. Do not re-enter unless a concrete new proposal or scheduled legislative action emerges.

2025-11-28.thesis: Trump policy action boosts nuclear contractors, with ELAT central
2025-11-28.assessment: Week 1 entry. The catalyst is Trump policy action boosting nuclear contractors, with ELAT as Citron Research's 'most important company in America' callout (Nov 22). No specific executive order or policy has resolved this yet — it remains a forward-looking, structural-aesthetic driver. The nuclear sector saw a pullback this week (OKLO -13%) driven by Ukraine-Russia peace proposal news and AI-capex rotation, NOT by any reversal of Trump's domestic nuclear stance. Those are different drivers. NuScale and LEU continue to get general favorable coverage as nuclear plays. The catalyst is still early and under-the-radar — the Citron piece just dropped days ago. No concrete next milestone is scheduled (no policy vote, no E.O. date), but as a structural policy shift it will continue generating new milestones through inauguration and early admin actions.
2025-11-28.exit_advice: HOLD. Trump nuclear policy is structural/open-ended. The sector pullback is geopolitical noise, not a catalyst reversal. ELAT remains the purest vehicle until the policy becomes concrete (E.O., contract award, regulatory action). Re-anchor if a specific milestone (e.g., E.O. signing, contract announcement) replaces the generalized 'policy action' framing.

2025-12-26.exit_case: IAEA Board session passed 5+ weeks ago with zero censure-escalation coverage — catalyst never fired.
2025-12-26.assessment: The IAEA Board of Governors session expected in late November has almost certainly convened by now — over a month ago with zero report of a censure vote or any follow-on Iranian retaliation. Five consecutive weeks of total silence on this specific catalyst strongly indicate the Western push either failed quietly or produced a toothless outcome. The escalation thesis — that a censure would trigger Iran to restrict inspectors/accelerate enrichment, tightening the uranium market — never fired. Meanwhile, U.S. military repositioning news (Okinawa-to-Guam) is unrelated China/Taiwan terrain, not Iran nuclear. Whatever the Board did, it did not produce the supply-shock catalyst we entered on.
2025-12-26.exit_advice: Hard exit. The IAEA Board session has passed with no reported censure escalation and no Iranian retaliation. Five weeks of total signal absence confirm the specific catalyst did not materialize as expected. Per silence-decay rules, conviction has decayed from entry to floor over five consecutive quiet weeks. Close all vehicles and redeploy capital to live, still-pending catalysts.

2025-12-26.thesis: Trump policy action boosts nuclear contractors, with ELAT central
2025-12-26.exit_case: Four silent weeks with no Trump nuclear policy action or scheduled milestone; weakened GOP position further clouds execution.
2025-12-26.assessment: Thesis drifts on pure speculation. No E.O., no DOE directive, no policy announcement, no scheduled vote — just the assumption that Trump will act on nuclear after taking office. ELAT has had zero press. The structural-aesthetic driver (pro-nuclear admin) is intact but generating no milestones. The Citron 'most important company' callout from Nov 22 has faded entirely from coverage. Conviction is at the edge of cull territory.
2025-12-26.exit_advice: HOLD but barely — structural driver (Trump admin nuclear policy) remains theoretically live with inauguration-era E.O. possibilities still ahead, but four consecutive weeks of zero ELAT-specific coverage and zero concrete policy action have compounded silence decay from ~6 to 3. The rebel GOP senator article suggests Trump's political capital is eroding, which weakens his ability to push ambitious pro-nuclear policy, though this is speculative not a reversal. The Putin/Belarus nuclear-capable missile deployment tangentially reinforces the broader nuclear narrative but does NOT directly address the Trump policy action catalyst. No concrete next milestone is scheduled or announced. One more silent week drops conviction to cull floor (2); a single fresh story on Trump nuclear E.O. signaling or ELAT-specific coverage resets higher.
